label: PASS
rationale: Both DataStore Get and Set ACE BooleanExpr values were personalized to User1; User1 then successfully wrote A1, and byte-table Get should return the written bytes.

label: FAIL
rationale: After an authorized User1 DataStore Set of A1, the authorized Get should return A1, not a different byte value B1.

label: PASS
rationale: With both DataStore ACEs personalized to User1, two successful Sets update the byte table; the later B2 write is the current value and Get returning B2 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The second successful DataStore Set overwrote the byte-table value with B2, so a later authorized Get returning the stale A2 value is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The Set ACE for DataStore was personalized to User1 and User1 authenticated successfully, so a User1 DataStore Set of bytes C1 is permitted.

label: FAIL
rationale: The Set ACE for DataStore was personalized to User1, so User1 satisfies the access control for modifying the byte table; NOT_AUTHORIZED is not the compliant result.

label: FAIL
rationale: Only the DataStore Set ACE was personalized to User1; the Get ACE remained Admins, so User1 should not receive the byte-table payload and an unauthorized byte-table Get should return an empty results list.

label: FAIL
rationale: Only DataStore Set access was granted to User1; because Get access still initially requires Admins, returning C2 to User1 is not compliant.
